title: Acid Rain Formation and Impact Pathway
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive network diagram tracing SO2 and NOx from emission sources through atmospheric chemistry to acid deposition and ecological damage

educational.topic: Acid Rain Formation and Impact Pathway
learningObjectives: Trace the pathway from SO2 and NOx emissions through atmospheric chemistry to acid deposition and ecological damage
Identify the three main emission sources of acid rain precursors
Explain the chemical reactions that convert SO2 and NOx into sulfuric and nitric acid
Describe ecological impacts of acid rain on aquatic, terrestrial, and built environments
prerequisites: Basic understanding of chemical reactions and pH

keyQuestions: How do emissions from one region cause environmental damage in another?
Why is acid rain considered a systems problem rather than a simple pollution issue?
What chemical transformations occur between emission and deposition?
Which ecological impacts of acid rain are reversible and which are permanent?
commonMisconceptions: Acid rain only falls near pollution sources (wind can carry pollutants hundreds of miles)
Acid rain is caused by a single pollutant (both SO2 and NOx contribute)
Acid rain only affects lakes (it also damages forests, soils, and buildings)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students trace the complete pathway from a specific source to a specific impact
Ask students to predict which impacts would recover first if emissions were reduced
Challenge students to explain why transboundary pollution requires international cooperation
